info.title: Azure Monitor Metrics Data API
info.description: Provides APIs for getting the metric data for Azure resources.

get.description: Lists the metric definitions for the resource.

parameters.name: metricnamespace
parameters.description: Metric namespace where the metrics you want reside.

get.description: Lists the metric namespaces for the resource.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: The ISO 8601 conform Date start time from which to query for metric namespaces.

get.description: **Lists the metric values for a resource**. This API used the [default ARM throttling limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/request-limits-and-throttling).

parameters.name: timespan
parameters.description: The timespan of the query. It is a string with the following format 'startDateTime_ISO/endDateTime_ISO'.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description:
The interval (i.e. timegrain) of the query in ISO 8601 duration format. Defaults to PT1M. Special case for 'FULL' value that returns single datapoint for entire time span requested.
*Examples: PT15M, PT1H, P1D, FULL*

parameters.name: metricnames
parameters.description: The names of the metrics (comma separated) to retrieve. Limit 20 metrics.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The list of aggregation types (comma separated) to retrieve.
*Examples: average, minimum, maximum*

parameters.name: top
parameters.description:
The maximum number of records to retrieve per resource ID in the request.
Valid only if filter is specified.
Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description:
The aggregation to use for sorting results and the direction of the sort.
Only one order can be specified.
*Examples: sum asc*

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description:
The **$filter** is used to reduce the set of metric data returned.
Example:
Metric contains metadata A, B and C.
- Return all time series of C where A = a1 and B = b1 or b2
**$filter=A eq ‘a1’ and B eq ‘b1’ or B eq ‘b2’ and C eq ‘*’**
- Invalid variant:
**$filter=A eq ‘a1’ and B eq ‘b1’ and C eq ‘*’ or B = ‘b2’**
This is invalid because the logical or operator cannot separate two different metadata names.
- Return all time series where A = a1, B = b1 and C = c1:
**$filter=A eq ‘a1’ and B eq ‘b1’ and C eq ‘c1’**
- Return all time series where A = a1
**$filter=A eq ‘a1’ and B eq ‘*’ and C eq ‘*’**.

parameters.name: resultType
parameters.description: Reduces the set of data collected. The syntax allowed depends on the operation. See the operation's description for details.

parameters.name: metricnamespace
parameters.description: Metric namespace where the metrics you want reside.

parameters.name: AutoAdjustTimegrain
parameters.description: When set to true, if the timespan passed in is not supported by this metric, the API will return the result using the closest supported timespan. When set to false, an error is returned for invalid timespan parameters. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: ValidateDimensions
parameters.description: When set to false, invalid filter parameter values will be ignored. When set to true, an error is returned for invalid filter parameters. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: rollupby
parameters.description: Dimension name(s) to rollup results by. For example if you only want to see metric values with a filter like 'City eq Seattle or City eq Tacoma' but don't want to see separate values for each city, you can specify 'RollUpBy=City' to see the results for Seattle and Tacoma rolled up into one timeseries.

get.description: Lists the metric definitions for the subscription.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: The region where the metrics you want reside.

parameters.name: metricnamespace
parameters.description: Metric namespace where the metrics you want reside.

get.description: **Lists the metric data for a subscription**. This API used the [default ARM throttling limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/request-limits-and-throttling).

post.description: **Lists the metric data for a subscription**. Parameters can be specified on either query params or the body. This API used the [default ARM throttling limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/request-limits-and-throttling).

parameters.description: The region where the metrics you want reside.

parameters.name: timespan

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The request body

AggregationType.description: The aggregation type of the metric.

ErrorContract.description: Common error response for all Azure Resource Manager APIs to return error details for failed operations. (This also follows the OData error response format.)

ErrorResponse.description: Describes the format of Error response.

message.description: Error message indicating why the operation failed.

Metric.description: The result data of a query.

type.description: The resource type of the metric resource.

name.description: The name and the display name of the metric, i.e. it is localizable string.

errorCode.description: 'Success' or the error details on query failures for this metric.

errorMessage.description: Error message encountered querying this specific metric.

timeseries.description: The time series returned when a data query is performed.

MetricAggregationType.description: The aggregation type of the metric.

MetricAvailability.description: Metric availability specifies the time grain (aggregation interval or frequency) and the retention period for that time grain.

timeGrain.description: The time grain specifies a supported aggregation interval for the metric. Expressed as a duration 'PT1M', 'P1D', etc.

retention.description: The retention period for the metric at the specified timegrain.  Expressed as a duration 'PT1M', 'P1D', etc.

MetricDefinition.description: Metric definition class specifies the metadata for a metric.

isDimensionRequired.description: Flag to indicate whether the dimension is required.

resourceId.description: The resource identifier of the resource that emitted the metric.

namespace.description: The namespace the metric belongs to.

name.description: The name and the display name of the metric, i.e. it is a localizable string.

category.description: Custom category name for this metric.

primaryAggregationType.description: The primary aggregation type value defining how to use the values for display.

supportedAggregationTypes.description: The collection of what aggregation types are supported.

metricAvailabilities.description: The collection of what aggregation intervals are available to be queried.

id.description: The resource identifier of the metric definition.

dimensions.description: The name and the display name of the dimension, i.e. it is a localizable string.

value.description: The values for the metric definitions.

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

MetricNamespace.description: Metric namespace class specifies the metadata for a metric namespace.

id.description: The ID of the metric namespace.

name.description: The escaped name of the namespace.

properties.description: Properties which include the fully qualified namespace name.

value.description: The values for the metric namespaces.

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

MetricNamespaceName.description: The fully qualified metric namespace name.

MetricResultType.description: Reduces the set of data collected. The syntax allowed depends on the operation. See the operation's description for details.

values.name: CountPerSecond
values.description: Rate unit of raw quantity per second.

values.name: BytesPerSecond
values.description: Rate unit of memory in bytes per second.

values.name: MilliSeconds
values.description: Unit of time in 1/1000th of a second.

values.name: ByteSeconds
values.description: Unit of data transfer or storage. It is the size of the data in bytes multiplied by the time it takes to transfer or store the data in seconds.

values.name: MilliCores
values.description: Unit of processing power in 1/1000th of a CPU core.

values.name: NanoCores
values.description: Unit of processing power in one billionth of a CPU core.

values.name: BitsPerSecond
values.description: Rate unit of binary digits per second.

timeStamp.description: The timestamp for the metric value in ISO 8601 format.

average.description: The average value in the time range.

minimum.description: The least value in the time range.

maximum.description: The greatest value in the time range.

total.description: The sum of all of the values in the time range.

count.description: The number of samples in the time range. Can be used to determine the number of values that contributed to the average value.

Response.description: The response to a metrics query.

cost.description: The integer value representing the relative cost of the query.

timespan.description: The timespan for which the data was retrieved. Its value consists of two datetimes concatenated, separated by '/'.  This may be adjusted in the future and returned back from what was originally requested.

interval.description:
The interval (window size) for which the metric data was returned in ISO 8601 duration format with a special case for 'FULL' value that returns single datapoint for entire time span requested (*Examples: PT15M, PT1H, P1D, FULL*).
This may be adjusted and different from what was originally requested if AutoAdjustTimegrain=true is specified. This is not present if a metadata request was made.

namespace.description: The namespace of the metrics being queried

resourceregion.description: The region of the resource being queried for metrics.

SubscriptionScopeMetricDefinition.description: Metric definition class specifies the metadata for a metric.

isDimensionRequired.description: Flag to indicate whether the dimension is required.

resourceId.description: The resource identifier of the resource that emitted the metric.

namespace.description: The namespace the metric belongs to.

name.description: The name and the display name of the metric, i.e. it is a localizable string.

category.description: Custom category name for this metric.

primaryAggregationType.description: The primary aggregation type value defining how to use the values for display.

supportedAggregationTypes.description: The collection of what aggregation types are supported.

metricAvailabilities.description: The collection of what aggregation intervals are available to be queried.

id.description: The resource identifier of the metric definition.

dimensions.description: The name and the display name of the dimension, i.e. it is a localizable string.

value.description: The values for the metric definitions.

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

SubscriptionScopeMetricsRequestBodyParam.description: Query parameters can also be specified in the body, specifying the same parameter in both the body and query parameters will result in an error.

timespan.description: The timespan of the query. It is a string with the following format 'startDateTime_ISO/endDateTime_ISO'.

interval.description:
The interval (i.e. timegrain) of the query in ISO 8601 duration format. Defaults to PT1M. Special case for 'FULL' value that returns single datapoint for entire time span requested.
*Examples: PT15M, PT1H, P1D, FULL*

metricNames.description: The names of the metrics (comma separated) to retrieve.

aggregation.description: The list of aggregation types (comma separated) to retrieve.

filter.description:
The **$filter** is used to reduce the set of metric data returned.
Example:
Metric contains metadata A, B and C.
- Return all time series of C where A = a1 and B = b1 or b2
**$filter=A eq ‘a1’ and B eq ‘b1’ or B eq ‘b2’ and C eq ‘*’**
- Invalid variant:
**$filter=A eq ‘a1’ and B eq ‘b1’ and C eq ‘*’ or B = ‘b2’**
This is invalid because the logical or operator cannot separate two different metadata names.
- Return all time series where A = a1, B = b1 and C = c1:
**$filter=A eq ‘a1’ and B eq ‘b1’ and C eq ‘c1’**
- Return all time series where A = a1
**$filter=A eq ‘a1’ and B eq ‘*’ and C eq ‘*’**.

top.description:
The maximum number of records to retrieve.
Valid only if $filter is specified.
Defaults to 10.

orderBy.description:
The aggregation to use for sorting results and the direction of the sort.
Only one order can be specified.
Examples: sum asc.

rollUpBy.description: Dimension name(s) to rollup results by. For example if you only want to see metric values with a filter like 'City eq Seattle or City eq Tacoma' but don't want to see separate values for each city, you can specify 'RollUpBy=City' to see the results for Seattle and Tacoma rolled up into one timeseries.

resultType.description: Reduces the set of data collected. The syntax allowed depends on the operation. See the operation's description for details.

metricNamespace.description: Metric namespace where the metrics you want reside.

autoAdjustTimegrain.description: When set to true, if the timespan passed in is not supported by this metric, the API will return the result using the closest supported timespan. When set to false, an error is returned for invalid timespan parameters. Defaults to false.

validateDimensions.description: When set to false, invalid filter parameter values will be ignored. When set to true, an error is returned for invalid filter parameters. Defaults to true.

TimeSeriesElement.description: A time series result type. The discriminator value is always TimeSeries in this case.

metadatavalues.description: The metadata values returned if $filter was specified in the call.

data.description: An array of data points representing the metric values.  This is only returned if a result type of data is specified.

Azure.ResourceManager.ResourceUriParamet.name: resourceUri
Azure.ResourceManager.ResourceUriParamet.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.
